description: In a kitchen, Phoebe sits at a round wooden table, resting her head on one hand and looking down at a small covered pot. She says, "You know, they say a watched pot never beeps."
Phoebe lifts her head, exhales audibly, and stands up, turning away from the table.
Phoebe walks toward Monica, who stands nearby. Looking off to the side, Phoebe says, "You know, it's been a couple hours and she hasn't called."
Phoebe turns her gaze back toward Monica.
Phoebe touches her face briefly, then says, "Not that I even care."
"Don't," she adds, shaking her head slightly.
Monica looks steadily back at Phoebe.
Monica crosses her arms and asks, "Why don't you just call her? You obviously want to."
Phoebe crosses her arms and replies, "You think you know me so well." Monica leans forward slightly and asks, "Don't you"
"...wanna?" Monica finishes. Looking downward, Phoebe murmurs, "Yeah."
Phoebe remains with her head lowered and arms crossed.
The word "It" appears in the subtitles, though no speech is clearly audible. Phoebe lifts her head slightly.
Monica nods and states, "Okay. I do know you." Phoebe looks up and replies, "That's what I said."
Phoebe rubs her nose with her hand and looks away.
Monica gestures with her hand and asks, "Well, so?" Phoebe replies, "I can't."
Phoebe uncrosses her arms and gestures as she speaks, "I can't. She dumped me. I totally trusted her."
Phoebe raises her hands and continues, "And then one day it was like okay, bye, phoebes gone," emphasizing her words with arm movements.
Phoebe crosses her arms again as Monica looks at her silently.
Phoebe lowers her voice slightly as she says, "You know what the saddest part is?"
She continues, "When we were playing together, that was, like, the most fun I've ever had in all my lives."
The scene changes to a nighttime exterior view of a city street, then shifts to the interior of a coffee shop.
Inside the coffee shop, a woman named Leslie sits on a stool on a small stage, playing an acoustic guitar and singing into a microphone, "My favorite shoes."
Leslie continues singing while strumming the guitar, "So good to me. I wear them every day..."
She goes on, "...down at the heel, holes in the toes. Don't care what..."
"...people say," she finishes the line.
Changing chords, Leslie sings, "My feet, best friend house to the end with them."
She continues, "I'm one hot chicky, though. Late one..."
"...night not much light. I stepped in something icky," Leslie sings. Phoebe walks onto the stage and stands beside the microphone.
Phoebe smiles broadly, holding a scarf and gloves in her hands as she prepares to sing.
Phoebe leans into the microphone and sings along with Leslie, "sticky shoes, sticky shoes always make me smile."
They continue singing together in harmony, "Sticky shoes, sticky shoes next time I"
The women pause their singing, smiling as Leslie continues to strum the guitar.
Phoebe and Leslie sing the final line together, "...avoid the pile," holding the last note.
The audience applauds. Phoebe laughs and embraces Leslie on the stage.
The scene changes to a daytime exterior view of a large brick building with a black cube structure in front, then shifts to the interior of a lecture hall.

In the lecture hall, Rachel takes notes while Ross sits beside her, resting his head on his hand. An off-screen male speaker says, "We're beginning to see a lot of layering of sheer fabrics and colors."
The speaker continues, "For instance, navy blouse..." as Ross turns his head to look at Rachel.
As the speaker's voice fades, saying, "...over a pink body," Ross leans toward Rachel and whispers, "I'm really glad we came."
Rachel smiles, turning to look at Ross, and rests her hand briefly on his arm.
The off-screen speaker says, "...Runways are totally here..." while Ross continues gazing at Rachel and whispers, "you're so pretty."
Rachel maintains her gaze on Ross, her smile widening slightly.
The speaker's voice returns with, "Providing slips for..." before Ross whispers, "I love you."
Rachel's eyes widen and she covers her mouth with her hand. Ross immediately covers his own mouth, his eyes darting away. The scene changes back to the coffee shop.
In the coffee shop, Phoebe sits on a couch playing her guitar and sings, "Smelly cat, smelly cat what are they feeding you?" Leslie sits beside her, listening.
Phoebe continues playing and sings the next line, "Smelly cat, smelly cat it's not your fault," as Leslie watches her closely.
Phoebe finishes the chord progression. Leslie points at her, her eyes widened.
Leslie gestures with her hand and says, "wow, that's great." Phoebe smiles and asks, "Oh, yeah?"
Leslie continues, "You know, you could totally sell this. It'd be perfect for, like, a kitty litter..."
"...campaign," Leslie finishes, nodding.
Phoebe's smile fades and she looks at Leslie with her jaw slightly open.
Phoebe shakes her head, saying, "A jingle? No. No, no, no." Leslie counters, "Why not? You make a ton of money."
Phoebe rests her hand on the guitar and replies, "Okay, well, if I was in this for the money, I would be a millionaire by now."
Leslie looks down briefly, adjusting her posture.
Phoebe gestures with one hand and says, "You just got to get out of that jingle head, sweetie."
Leslie raises her hands with her palms facing outward and nods.
Leslie concedes, "You're right."
"I'm sorry," Leslie says. "That's okay," Phoebe replies, her volume softer than her previous line.
Phoebe positions her hands on the guitar strings and says, "All right, I'm going to play a song now that's really, really sad. Okay?"
Phoebe closes her eyes, tilts her head slightly, and announces, "It's called Magician Box Mix up."
Phoebe opens her eyes and begins strumming the guitar as the segment ends.

In a dimly lit auditorium, an audience is seated. Ross sits with his eyes closed and his head resting against the seat back, while Rachel sits next to him holding a notepad and pen. A man speaks off-screen.
Rachel writes on her notepad. The off-screen speaker says, "Oversized bracelets, oversized earrings..."
Ross jolts awake, his head snapping up as he makes a brief vocalization. The speaker finishes, "...oversizing of accessories in general is very popular now." Ross looks around quickly, his eyes wide.
Ross settles back into his seat, resting his head on his hand. Rachel's gaze remains fixed on him. Audience laughter is audible.
The speaker continues, "Garments with a Far Eastern influence really breaking out this year." Ross shifts slightly in his seat.
The scene changes to an apartment kitchen. Chandler sits at a counter reading a newspaper. The door opens.
Monica enters through the door and says, "Hi."
Monica walks toward the counter holding a glass bottle. "Hey. Okay, more of this. My milk's gone bad," she says.
Chandler glances up from the newspaper and replies, "Oh, I hate that. I once had a thing. A half and half."
Chandler looks back down at the newspaper and adds, "Stole my car."
Monica sets the milk bottle on the counter. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica asks, "So how was your date with Ginger?"
Chandler looks up and replies, "Great."
Chandler stands up rapidly.
Chandler says, "It was great."
Chandler walks away from the counter, gesturing with one hand.
Pacing slightly, Chandler says, "She's great. Great looking, great personality."
Chandler stops walking and says, "She's greatness."
Monica places a hand on the milk bottle.
Monica looks at Chandler and says, "Sounds like she's got the whole package."
Chandler stares at Monica.
Chandler nods and says, "Oh, yeah."
Monica smiles slightly.
Gesturing with one hand, Monica asks, "No, she's got it, huh?"
Chandler lowers his gaze, clasping his hands together.
Monica says, "She's the real deal."
Chandler looks back up at Monica.
Chandler steps closer and says, "Joey told you about the," pausing as Monica nods, "uh huh."
Chandler raises a hand to his chest.
Chandler gestures with his hands and says, "Oh, God. It freaked me out, okay? I know it shouldn't have, but it did. I mean, I like her. I don't want to"
Chandler continues, "stop seeing her, but every so often it's like, hey, you know what? Where's your leg?" He points down toward his own leg.
Chandler gestures outward with both hands. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler points his thumbs at his chest and says, "I mean, I'm the smallest person in the world, aren't I? I'm the smallest person in the world."
Joey walks out of a hallway wearing a robe and says, "Morning."
Chandler points his thumb toward Joey and says, "Actually, he's the smallest person in the world."
Joey stops walking, his mouth parting slightly. Audience laughter begins.
Joey rubs his chest with one hand while Chandler watches him. Laughter continues.
Joey looks at Chandler and says, "Heard about the leg burning?"
Chandler keeps his gaze on Joey. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler replies, "It came up."
Joey looks down, his brow furrowing.
Joey looks back up and says, "Listen, I know it's a long shot, but"
Joey raises one hand.
Joey continues, "by any chance, did she find that funny?"
Chandler stares at Joey. Joey shrugs. Chandler turns and walks out of the frame. The scene changes to the exterior of a brick apartment building.
Ross walks into the apartment carrying a paper bag and says, "So I nodded off a little."
Rachel follows him inside and says, her volume rising on the last word, "Nodded off?"
Rachel unbuttons her coat and says, "Ross, you were snoring. My father's boat didn't make that much noise when it hit rocks."
Ross gestures with his hands and says, "Come on, 45 minutes."
Ross continues, "45 minutes. The man talked about strappy back dresses."
Rachel slips off her bag strap.
Rachel leans forward slightly and says, "Okay, how about 4 hours in a freezing museum auditorium listening to Professor Pitt Stains and his hey,"
Rachel mimics holding something small between her fingers and says, "everybody, remember that thing that's been dead for a gazillion years? Well, here's that little bone we didn't know it had."
Rachel walks toward the couch and drapes her coat over the back. Ross watches her.
Ross points a finger at Rachel and says, "First of all, it's Professor Pitain."
Ross holds up another finger.
Ross gestures with his hands and says, "And second of all, that little bone proved that that particular dinosaur had wings but didn't fly."
Rachel raises a hand to her chest and responds, "Okay, see, now what I just"
Rachel flaps her hand rapidly near her shoulder and says at a fast pace, "heard. Blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah."
Ross stares at Rachel, blinking slowly.
Ross walks closer to Rachel and says, "A hundred million people went to see a movie about what I do. I wonder how many people would go see a movie called"
Ross points his finger forward.
Ross finishes, "Jurassic Parka."
Rachel closes her eyes and exhales audibly.
Rachel looks at Ross and says, "No, a bunch of out of control jackets take over an island."
Ross stares at Rachel. She turns her head away. Audience laughter is audible.
In a kitchen, Rachel stands facing Ross with her arms relaxed by her sides.
Rachel gestures with her hands as she asks, "You know, if what I do is so lame, then why did you insist on coming with me this morning? Was" Ross stands across from her, lowering his head slightly.
Rachel asks, "it so I just wouldn't go with Mark?" Ross shakes his head, looks away, and replies, "No,"
Ross steps forward and looks down.
Ross looks up at Rachel and says softly, "I wanted to be with you."
Rachel crosses her arms.
Ross gestures with his hands as he says, "I don't know, I feel like lately I feel like you're slipping away from me with this new job and all these new people"
Ross points toward her, saying, "and got this whole other life going on."
Ross opens his hands and continues, "And I know it's dumb, but I just hate that"
Ross lowers his hands and finishes, "I'm not a part of it."
Rachel unfolds her arms, stepping closer to Ross and looking at him.
Rachel places her hands on his chest and says, "It's not dumb."
Rachel keeps her hands on Ross's chest, tilting her head as she looks at him.
Rachel says, "But maybe it's okay that you're not a part of it. You know what I mean?"
Ross stares at her with a slightly furrowed brow.
Rachel gestures with her hands as she explains, "I mean, it's like, I like that you're not involved in that part of my life."
Ross continues looking at her, remaining still.
Ross replies, "That's a little clearer," as audience laughter is heard.
Rachel reaches up to hold Ross's neck.
Rachel holds his shoulders and neck, saying, "honey. See, it doesn't mean that I don't love you, because I do. I love you. I love you so much."
Rachel lowers her hands to his chest.
Rachel gestures to her chest and says, "But my work, it's for me."
Rachel holds her hands up, palms facing him, and says, "I'm out there on my own and I'm doing it. And it's scary, but I love it because it's mine."
Ross maintains eye contact with Rachel.
Rachel asks, "But I mean, is that okay?"
Ross smiles slightly and says, "Sure."
Ross hugs Rachel tightly. The audience makes a collective "aww" sound, followed by laughter.
The setting changes to an apartment. Chandler and Ginger sit across from each other at a small table, eating with chopsticks.
They chew their food in silence as Chandler looks at Ginger.
Ginger lowers her chopsticks slightly and asks, "You're thinking about my leg, aren't you?"
Chandler stops chewing, his eyes widening. He gestures with his hand and says, "No, actually, I forgot. What is the deal with that again?" Audience laughter is heard.
Ginger puts her chopsticks down on her plate.
Ginger folds her hands and says, "It's okay if it bothers you."
Ginger stands up and steps away from the table, saying, "Really. I mean, the only thing I need to know is how much it bothers you because I don't like wasting my time."
Chandler stands up to face her.
Ginger looks at Chandler and asks, "Am I wasting my time?"
Chandler steps closer to Ginger.
Chandler says, "No, I don't think so."
Ginger smiles, steps toward him, and replies, "Good. It's just like anything else. You just have to get used to it."
Ginger and Chandler kiss. Chandler puts his arms around her, and they continue kissing as they move toward a black leather recliner.
They fall into the recliner together, Chandler sitting and Ginger straddling his lap as they keep kissing.
Ginger breaks the kiss, moves her hand to Chandler's chest, and unbuttons his shirt.
Ginger touches his exposed chest and asks, "What's that?"
Chandler looks down at his chest.
Chandler replies, "That's my nubbin."
Ginger stares at his chest with her brow furrowed, keeping her hand in place.
Ginger asks, "What's a nubbin?"
Chandler raises his eyebrows and opens his mouth slightly.
Chandler explains, "It's kind of a third nipple kind of thing."
Ginger's eyes widen.
Ginger asks, "You have three nipples?" Chandler replies, "Well, two regulars"
Chandler gestures slightly with his hand.
Chandler continues, "and one that barely qualifies as a" while Ginger suddenly pushes off of him and stands up.
Ginger walks briskly toward the kitchen counter, leaving Chandler sitting in the recliner with his mouth open.
Chandler asks, "what?" and Ginger replies, "Nothing."
Ginger picks up her coat from a stool.
Ginger hurriedly puts her coat on and says, "I just remembered I have to leave."
Chandler stands up, adjusting his shirt, and walks toward her.
Chandler asks, "You have to leave? Now?"
Chandler asks, "How come?" Ginger adjusts her scarf, stammering, "Well, it's nubbin. Nothing." Audience laughter is heard.
Ginger turns away from him and walks toward the apartment door.
Ginger stops by the door and says, "You know what?"
Ginger continues, "I'll see you later."
Ginger opens the door, steps into the hallway, and quickly pulls the door shut behind her. Chandler stands staring at the door.
Chandler exhales and says, "Okay. Whew."
A sequence of brief, silent portrait images of Rachel, Ross, Chandler, and Ginger is shown.
In a coffee shop, Phoebe walks up to Leslie, who is holding a red guitar. Patrons sit in the background as soft instrumental music plays.
Phoebe stops in front of Leslie. "I thought you were coming. Where were you?" she asks.
Leslie raises her index finger toward Phoebe.
Leslie says, "Okay, don't get mad, okay?" Phoebe gestures with her hands and responds, "Don't give me a reason to get mad, okay?"
Leslie lowers her hand.
Leslie gestures with both hands, saying, "I played Smelly Cat for the people of my old ad agency. They went nuts."
Phoebe shakes her head, pointing to herself. "No, look, I told you that I didn't want you to try and sell it," she says.
Phoebe gestures with quick arm movements and adds, "and you big fat did it anyway."
Leslie touches her own chest.
Phoebe says, "Good. You know what? I think five years ago, I probably would have done anything to play with you."
Phoebe points to herself, then to Leslie. "But I can do it by myself, right? And if"
Phoebe continues, "I can't trust you, then just forget it." Leslie shakes her head and replies, "No, I don't want to forget it. Okay, you know what?"
Leslie points her finger at Phoebe and says, "You have to choose. All right?"
Leslie continues, "If the most important thing on the planet to you is this cat poopy thing then"
Phoebe's gaze remains on Leslie.
Leslie drops her hand and says, "okay, you can have smelly cats. But we won't be partners."
Phoebe remains silent, keeping her eyes on Leslie.
Leslie asks, "So what's it going to be?" The setting changes to a television screen showing a woman in a bathroom with a cat as a jingle plays. "Smelly cat problem?" an announcer says.
The woman on the television waves her hand in front of her nose. The announcer continues, "Odor in the litter box?"
The television displays a cat sitting next to a box of cat litter. "Don't change your kitty."
The announcer finishes, "Change your kitty litter."
The setting changes to an apartment living room. Phoebe sits playing a red guitar. Monica stands nearby, pointing a remote control at the television.
Monica lowers the remote as the television turns off, then turns toward Phoebe. Joey sits on the arm of the sofa, looking at Phoebe.
Monica says, "Sorry, Phoebe."
Joey says, "Yeah," and Phoebe replies, "Okay."
Phoebe adjusts her hands on the guitar.
Phoebe gently strums the guitar and says, "Yeah, I actually am. Yeah, because, you know, life's going to hand you all kinds of stuff."
Phoebe looks up from the guitar.
Phoebe says, "You learn your little lessons, and hopefully you grow."
Monica stands near the television, watching Phoebe.
Phoebe asks, "Want to hear a new song?"
Joey and Monica respond, "Yeah, okay."
Joey and Monica quickly move to sit on the floor directly in front of Phoebe.
Phoebe plays the guitar and sings, "Jingle bitch screwed me over"
Joey and Monica's eyes widen as they look at Phoebe.
Phoebe strums with fast movements and sings loudly, "go to hell jingle whore go to hell, go to hell, go to"
Phoebe strikes a final chord and stops playing.
Phoebe says, "that's all I have so far."
Joey and Monica remain seated on the floor in silence, looking toward Phoebe.
The setting changes to the apartment kitchen and living area. The front door opens.
Chandler walks inside. Joey turns his head toward him and asks, "Where have you"
Rachel, Ross, and Monica turn their attention toward the door.
Joey finishes, "where have you been?" Chandler replies, "The doctor." Ross asks, "Is everything okay?"
Chandler says, "Oh, yes. Just had me a little nubonectomy."
Chandler places his hands on his hips.
Chandler says, "Yep. Two nipples, no waiting." Rachel smiles, and Monica says, "Wow."
Ross looks at Rachel and says, "Like Rachel in high school."
Rachel's smile fades as she stares at Ross.
Rachel asks, "What?"
Ross smiles slightly and tilts his head.
Ross says, "Come on, I was kidding. It was such an obvious joke."
Chandler points at Ross and speaks rapidly, "That was an obvious joke, and I didn't think of it."
Chandler lowers his hand. "Why didn't I think of it? The source of all my powers."
Chandler's eyebrows draw together in a frown.
Chandler looks down at his chest and says softly, "Oh, dear God. What have I done?"
The screen becomes completely black.
The screen remains black.
